lines: Ern-OS
a small operating system written
entirely in plain-English Ernos
This looks like the very first start, so let me prepare the disk...
The disk is ready.
Ern-OS needs to know one person before we begin - you.
You will be the administrator of this machine.
What should I call you? (letters and digits only)
Choose a password (at least 4 characters):
Pleased to meet you, maria. Your home is /home/maria.
Who are you?
Your password?
Welcome back, maria.
-- Welcome to Ern-OS --
Hello, maria. This machine is yours: everything it knows
lives in its own disk folder, works without any network, and
every part of it - including the system itself - is written in
plain English.
You are standing in your home folder. Some things to try:
make a folder called letters
write a note called hello saying good morning
show my files
open notes        - a small editor for changing notes
open files        - walk your folders by number
open monitor      - how the system is doing
what is running   - the services working in the background
help              - everything Ern-OS understands
Whenever you are done, say 'log out' - or 'shut down' to stop
the machine. Nothing is ever forgotten between visits.
Say 'help' if you would like to know what I understand.
/home/maria > help
Here is what you can say to me:
Finding your way
where am i             - which folder you are standing in
show my files          - what is in this folder
look in letters        - what is in another folder
go to letters          - step into a folder
go up                  - step out to the folder above
go home                - back to your home folder
Making and changing things
make a folder called letters
write a note called hello saying good morning
read hello             - show what a note says
move hello to greeting - rename or move something
remove hello           - throw something away
Apps and services
open notes             - change a note line by line
open files             - walk your folders by number
open monitor           - how the system is doing
open welcome           - the first-day tour
what is running        - services and apps
start the heartbeat service   (admins)
stop the heartbeat service    (admins)
People (admins)
add a person called finn
list people
remove the person called finn
About you and the system
who am i               - your name and role
what time is it
help                   - this list
log out                - let someone else use the machine
shut down              - stop Ern-OS
Rebuilding itself (admins)
rebuild the system     - recompile Ern-OS from its own source
rebuild the toolchain  - re-bake the vendored compiler with clang
restart                - shut down so the new system boots next
/home/maria > say hello world
I do not know how to 'say'. Say 'help' to see what I understand.
/home/maria > look in /home/maria
/home/maria is empty.
/home/maria > shut down
Ern-OS is going to sleep. Goodbye.
